California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 
■7′  ■990
Tommie L. schuette
925 seacoast Drive
Imperial Beach, cA 92032
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-9 0-6 00
Dear Ms. schuette:
You have requested advice concerning application of the
conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political' Reform Act (the
Act")1to your duties as a councilmember of the city of Imperial
Beach. You were aLso a candidate for mayor in the November, 1990
election, 
The followj-ng advice is based upon the facts Provided
in your letter and upon several telephone conversatj-ons with
Mr. Lynn McDougaL, the city Attorney. Since your letter did not
provide sufficient facts about pending decisions, t e are unable to
render specific advice. Therefore, t^le are treatinq your request
as one for informal- assistance.z
OUESTION
Under the Act, may you participate in decisions regarding the
Seacoast specific plan in view of the fact that your real estate
company leases office space and Inanages two properties in the
specific plan area?
CONCLUSION
You may not participate in any decision regarding the
Seacoast sp-cific plan if the decision will have a material
financial effect on any of your economic interests.
FACTS
You are a licensed real estate broker and a partner in Bahia
Realty, r"rhich handles both real, estate sales and property manaqe-
ment. Bahia Real-ty leases office space in a mixed use (office and
・  Government Code Sections 8■
000-9■0■5。  All statutory references
are to the Covernment Code un■
ess otherwise indicated.  CommiSSion
regu■ations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section

■ references to regulations are to Tit■

immunity provided by an opinion
83■■4, Regulation 
■8329(c)(3)。
)
428 J Street,Suite 800 ● P.0.Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804‐ 0807 ● (916)322´ 5660


Fife No. I-9 o-5 0 0
Page 2
residential) building at 925 Seacoast Dri've, which is in the
seacoast specific pLan area. You manage some residential property
j,n this building, for which you derive incorne of over $250 a year-
You also manage an apartment building in the specific plan
area, for which you derive approxinately $5,000 a year in income.
Since this is an older building, the plan could call for the
dernolition of these apartments. To date, your office has not been
the agent for any sates in the specific plan area.
The Seacoast specific plan hras adopted by the city council in
l-984 and approved by the coastal, conmission in 1985. The plan
included a iedevetopnent aqency which was l-ater voted down by the
cj-tj-zens of the cornrnunity. A referendum for a new plan will be on
the ballot in the upcoming election. 
If it is defeated by the
voters, the city councj.l $riI1 have to adopt a new p1an. If it is
approved by the voters, the city council is entitLed to amend the
plan.
ANALYSI S
The Act prohibits a public official 
from making, participat-
i.ng in, or uslng her official 
position to influence a governmental
deci-sion in which she knows or has reason to know she has a
financial j.nterest. 
(Section 87100.) A public official 
has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision r,rill have a naterial financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the
of f j.cj.il- or a member of her irnmediate farnily or on, among other
things:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investrnent worth
one thousand dollars ($1000) or nore.
(b) Any real property in which the public
official, has a direct or indirect interest torth
one thousand dollars ($1000) or nore.
(c) Any source of incone, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terrns available to the public srithout regard to
official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty 
do1-
lars ($250) or more in value provided to, received
by or promised to the public official within 12
rnonths prior to the t j.me vrhen the dec j.sion is made -
(d) Any business entity in which the publi-c
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of nanagement.


Fl■e No. I-90-600
Page 3
(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
t\.ro hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
officiat within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is nade.
Section 87103 (a) - (e) .
As a public official, 
you may not participate in a decision
if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decj-sion will have a
material financial effect, distinguishabLe from the effect on the
publj.c generally, on your real estate business, your leasehold
interesi, or on any source of income of $250 or more within the 12
nonths prior to the tine when the decision is made. (section
87103 (aj , (b) , (c) and (d) . ) Income of an individual includes a
pro rata share of any income of a business in which the individual
owns a 10 percent interest or greater. 
(Section 82030(a).)
Therefore, as a partner in Bahia Realty, a source of income to
your real estate company is also considered a source of incone to
you .
Foreseeab i l itv
The effects of a decision are reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantiaL likelihood that they wilL occur. To be foresee-
able, the effects of a deci,sion must be nore than a mere possibil-
ityr' however, certainty is not required. 
(Downev cares v. Downev
Communitv Developrnent Con. (1987) 196 cal.App- 3d 983, 989-99Li
Ei!! ". UgIIgti (1s77) 70 ca1.APp.3d 817, 822i In re Thorner (1975)
f fppc ops. 198.) The Act seeks to prevent more than actual
conflict! of interest, it seeks to prevent even the appearance of
a possible conflict of j,nterest. (witt v. llorroer, supra, at 823')
Mater ia l itv
ReguLation 18702 (copy encl'osed) sets forth the guidelines
for det6rruining whether an officialrs 
economic interest in a deci-
sion is "material)-yir affected as required by Section 87103' If
the official's 
finlncial interest is directlv involved in the
decision, Regulation L8702.1 (copy enclosed) applies to deterrnine
nateriality. 
on the other hand, if the official's 
financial
interest i-s indirectlv affected by the decision, Regulations
L87O2.Z to :.e702.6 (copies enclosed) apply to determine whether
the effect of the decision is rnaterial.
since we were not provided with specific facts about the
Seacoast specific plan ind how your econornic interests may be af-
i."t"a, 
tn'e eof fow-ing is a general discussion regarding material-
ity and disqualificaiion. 
You manage two properties in the
splcific plJn area and derive j-ncome of over $250 a year from the
oiner= of each property. Therefore, the owners of each property
ir"-i 
=""."e of inlorne to you. (Section 87103(c),) If a decision
regarding the specific plan has a naterial financial effect'


File No. r-9 0-600
Page 4
distinguishabLe from the effect on the public generally, on an
individual or a business entity which is a source of income to
you, you must disqualify yourself from participating in such deci-
sion.
If an individual is a source of income to you or Bahia
Realty, Regulation L87o2.6 (b) provides that the effect of a deci-
sion is material if:
(a) The decision will affect the individual's
income, investments, or other tangible or
intangible assets or liabilities 
(other than real
property) by $1,000 or more; or
(b) The decision will affect the individual's
real property interest in a nanner that is
considered material under section 18702.3 or Sec-
tion 18702.4.
Thus, pursuant to Regulation L8702.3, you may not participate
in any city- council decision regarding the specific Plan which
will iave I reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on
the owner of the property $rho is a source of income to you. For
exarnple, if the specific plan ca1ls for the demol-ition of the
apartment building, it would appear that the owner \{i11 be materi-
aily affected. A6cordingly, you Bust disqualify frorn participat-
ing in such decision.
Likewise, if a business entity is a source of income to you
or Bahia Rea1iy, you may not participate in any decision regarding
the specific plan lrhich witl have a reasonably foreseeable rnate-
ria] tinancial effect on the business entity. 
In this regard,
Regulations La7o2.L and 18702.2 should be reviewed to ascertain if
th6re will be a material financial effect on the business entity'
You also may not participate in any decision regarding the
specific ptan which will have a naterial financial effect on your
lLasehold- interest. 
Bahia Realty has a five-year lease for its
office aE 925 seacoast Drive, which is in the specific plan area'
pursuant to Regulation 18702.4, the effect of a decision is mate-
rial as to youi leasehold interest if any of the following ap-
plies:
(a) The decision will change the legaIIy aI-
Iovrabl-e use of the leased property, and the lessee
has a right to sublease the property;
(b) It is reasonably foreseeable that the
Lessee will chanqe the actual use of the property
as a result of the decision;


File No. I-90-600
Page 5
(c) It is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will result j,n a change in the actual use
of property within 300 feet of the leased property,
and the changed use will- significantly 
enhance or
significantly 
decrease the use or enj oyrnent of the
Ieased property;
(d) The decision will increase or decrease
the amount of rent for the leased property by S250
or 5 percent, whichever is greater, during any 12-
month period following the decision; or
(e) The decision will result in a change in
the temination date of the lease .
You are further precluded from Participating in any decision
regarding the specific plan if it wil,l have a rnaterial financial
efiect on your real estate comPany. If your company eri1l be af-
fected by any of the specific plan decisions, the effect is
consj.dered material under Regulation 18702'2(g)5 if:
(1) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease in the gross revenues for a fiscal year
of S10,000 or nore; or
(2) The decision will- result in the business
entity incurring or avoiding additional expenses or
reduci,ng or elininating existing expenses for a
fiscal year in the amount of $2,500 or more r' or
(3) The decision wil-I result in an increase
or decrease in the value of assets or liabilities
of $10, o00 or more.
Therefore, it is incumbent upon you to determine if any of
your economic interests will be materially affected by the
Seacoast specific plan. If so, you must disqualify yourself from
participating in decisions regarding the specific plan.
Public Generallv
Even if the reasonably foreseeable financial effect of a
decision is material, disqualification is required only if the
effect is distinguishable from the effect on the public generally.
(Section 87103.) For the city, the public consists of all
residents of the city. 
Thus, disqualification is required unless
the specific plan decisions will affect any of your econornic
i-nterests in substantially the same manner as it will affect a1I
residents of the city, or a significant segment of the residents
of the city. 
Under the facts provided, the effect of the specific
3  This assumes that the financia■ size of your company is such
that it is covered by subdivision (g)。f 18702.2. If it is not′
please review the regu■ation to app■y the appropriate standard.


File No. I-90-600
Page 6
p■an decis■ons on your econom■
c ■nterests wi■■ not be the same as
the effect on the public genera■
■y.
I trust that thiS 
■etter has answered your questions.  If you
have any further questions regarding this matter′
 p■ease contact
me at (9■6)322-590■
.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Under the Act, may you participate in decisions regarding the
Seacoast specific plan in view of the fact that your real estate
company leases office space and Inanages two properties in the
specific plan area?

CONCLUSION: You may not participate in any decision regarding the
Seacoast sp-cific plan if the decision will have a material
financial effect on any of your economic interests.
